info.name: Innopolis PLM API
info.description:
Full API for the Innopolis BOM/PLM backend (FRD Modules 1-6).

Setup:
1. Set {{baseUrl}} (default http://localhost:7100).
2. Run Auth → Login (stores {{token}}). Switch the email/password to test other roles.
3. Run the folders top-to-bottom — create requests capture IDs into collection variables so the flow chains.

Note: numeric fields come back as JSON strings (parse with Number).

item.name: Auth
item.description: Run Login first — it stores the JWT in {{token}}. Login also returns data.must_change_password; if true the token is restricted (everything except set-password returns 403 PASSWORD_CHANGE_REQUIRED) until you call Set password.

request.description: Sets {{token}} for all other requests. Try other demo logins: admin@innopolis.bio/admin123, purchase@…/purchase123, commercial@…/commercial123, stores@…/stores123, management@…/management123.

Forced first-login change. Works ONLY while the account is flagged must_change (after admin create/reset). current_password = the temporary password. Returns a fresh, unrestricted token → captured into {{token}}.

item.name: User Management (Admin)
item.description: Administrator-only. Requires logging in as admin@innopolis.bio / admin123. password_hash is never returned. Passwords are admin-managed (temporary password + forced first-login change) — there is no routine self-service change.

request.description: Administrator only. The user logs in with this password directly — NO forced change on create (use Reset password to force one). initials auto-derived if omitted. Duplicate email → 409.
Profile fields only: name/email/role/team/hue/is_active. NO password here. Can't demote/deactivate the last Administrator or yourself.
Sets a temporary password + flags the user to change it at next login. Omit temporary_password to auto-generate one — it is returned ONCE in data.temporary_password for you to share.
Soft delete — the user can no longer log in. You cannot delete your own account.

item.name: Resource Specs
item.description: Predefined resource specification master. A material can reference several via resource_spec_ids[].

item.name: Materials (Parts)
item.description: Material code TT-SS-MM-DDDD is generated server-side from the selected masters.

request.description: A material can have multiple preferred vendors. Pass `vendor_ids` as an array of vendor UUIDs (each must already exist in the Vendor Database → run 'Create vendor' first so {{supplierId}} is set). Omit `vendor_ids` on PATCH to leave the list unchanged; send [] to clear it. `GET /api/parts/:id` returns `vendor_ids` and `preferred_vendors`.

body.raw:
{
  "code": "V-CUST1",
  "name": "Custom Vendor Pvt Ltd",
  "country": "India",
  "region": "Domestic",
  "category": "Mechanical Bought-out",
  "categories_supplied": [
    "Mechanical Bought-out"
  ],
  "tier": 2,
  "contact": "A. Kumar",
  "email": "sales@custom.in",
  "phone": "+91 20 1234 5678",
  "gst_vat": "27ABCDE1234F1Z5",
  "payment_terms": "30 days",
  "lead_time_avg": 18,
  "rating": 4.2,
  "on_time_pct": 91,
  "quality_pct": 95,
  "status": "Approved",
  "approved": true
}

request.description: Provide from_bom_id to copy a released BOM's lines, OR a `lines` array for ad hoc items.
RFQ must be Sent. One quotation per vendor. unit_price per rfq_line_id.
Draft→Pending Approval→Open→Partially Received→Received→Closed; Cancelled from any non-terminal.
Pass warehouse_id to post accepted stock. Rejected qty is recorded but never enters inventory.
